subject: [PATCH v5 5/5] sunrpc: derive the pool count instead of caching it in sv_nrpools

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: The reviewer pointed out that the if() condition in svc_pool_for_cpu() is always false because the values stored in svc_pool_map.to_pool are less than svc_pool_map.npools, but this does not affect the correctness of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
The values stored in svc_pool_map.to_pool are all less than
svc_pool_map.npools.
So that if() condition cannot be true. 

But those two things don't reduce the correctness of the patch.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks for doing this.
NeilBrown

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch removes the cached field sv_nrpools from svc_serv and instead derives the pool count using a new helper function svc_serv_nrpools(). The function uses the existing fields sv_is_pooled and svc_pool_map.npools to calculate the correct number of pools. This change is made possible by the fact that the pool mode is always per-node, making it safe to read npools without holding the mutex. The patch also removes a modulus operation from svc_pool_for_cpu(), which should improve performance.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed a concern about exporting svc_pool_map by explaining it's currently private to svc.c and cannot be exported.

Author acknowledged a redundant if statement in the code and agreed to send a follow-up patch to remove it, indicating that further revision is planned.
reviews.raw_body:
That would mean that we would have to export svc_pool_map, which is
currently private to svc.c.

---

Ahh good point. I guess we can remove that if statement. I can send
Chuck a follow-on patch. Thanks for the R-b!

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch removes the cached field sv_nrpools from svc_serv and instead derives the pool count using a new helper function svc_serv_nrpools(). The function uses the existing fields sv_is_pooled and svc_pool_map.npools to calculate the correct number of pools. This change is made possible by the fact that the pool mode is always per-node, making it safe to read npools without holding the mutex. The patch also removes a modulus operation from svc_pool_for_cpu(), which should improve performance.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown questioned why svc_pool_map is exported instead of svc_serv_nrpools, suggesting inlining or marking as 'inline' the function in svc_pool_for_cpu() to reduce export overhead.
reviews.raw_body:
Why is exporting svc_pool_map more problematic than exporting svc_serv_nrpools?
I think it would be good for at least the code in svc_pool_for_cpu() to
inline the function.  Maybe it already does?  Or maybe marking it
"inline" but still exporting it would work.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about increasing the interface surface between sunrpc.ko and nfsd.ko by exporting struct svc_pool_map, instead choosing to keep the interface small with a single accessor function.
reviews.raw_body:
Doing what you suggest would increase the interface "surface" between
sunrpc.ko and nfsd.ko.

Currently struct svc_pool_map is only defined in net/sunrpc/svc.c, so
we'd not only need to export the symbol, but also we'd have to make
that definition public.

Why expose so many internal details to other modules when all we need
is this one accessor? I think keeping this interface small is
preferable.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the hot path in svc_pool_for_cpu() may be impacted by the change, but does not have time to investigate further and leaves it as a potential future improvement.
reviews.raw_body:
Your point is certainly valid, and this _is_ a hot path. If we can
demonstrate that this matters, then I'd be happy to revisit the idea. I
don't have the cycles to do so at the moment though.

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch removes the cached field sv_nrpools from svc_serv and instead derives the pool count using a new helper function svc_serv_nrpools(). The function uses the existing fields sv_is_pooled and svc_pool_map.npools to calculate the correct number of pools. This change is made possible by the fact that the pool mode is always per-node, making it safe to read npools without holding the mutex. The patch also removes a modulus operation from svc_pool_for_cpu(), which should improve performance.

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that removing the cached field and replacing it with a helper function to derive the pool count has an abstract benefit (interface size) but may have a concrete cost (code size), and requested further consideration of this trade-off.
reviews.raw_body:
In general, yes.  But it is not without cost.  Sometimes the cost
matters.
Interface size has an abstract value.  Code size can have concrete
value.  Comparing them is subjective I guess.
I have no concrete evidence that there is a problem, so I'll stop
objecting.

Thanks,
NeilBrown
